title: Level 250
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:<span style="font-family: IM Fell English; text-shadow:0px 0px 5px #FFFFFF; line-height:1.4; color:#FFFFFF; background-color:#000000; padding:10px;">The Aion´s Purgatory}}

{{CSS|User:阁楼福克123/Darkness.css}}

{{Rewrite in Progress
|reason=It could be better.
|author=A_RandomMax
|rewriter=A_RandomMax
|progress=85% (Temporarily Paused)
}}

<center>
{{Audio|file=REPULSIVE-Akane s-Regret.mp3|align=center}}

{{Content Warning|bad_content=descriptions of suicide|page=
<div style="font-family: IM Fell English; text-shadow:0px 0px 5px #FFFFFF; line-height:1.4; color:#FFFFFF; background-color:#000000; padding:10px;">
{{Image Banner|image=BlueSky10070316-c-f3dddad-scaled.jpg|caption=It´s a beatiful day outside, isn´t it?}}

I’ve always hated math.

Everything had to fit. Every number had a place, every equation a solution... not really my type.

That evening, I was lying on my bed, surrounded by notebooks and formulas, studying for a math test. Just another [[Level 398|ordinary day]]. Everything felt normal. So calm, so peaceful.

From the kitchen, I could hear my mother preparing dinner, the usual sounds of pots clinking and something sizzling on the stove.

The smell of food was starting to drift into my room. It was warm, familiar.

At some point, I got up to go to the bathroom. I didn´t think twice about it and I stood up quickly, so I tripped down and hurt my head.

I heard a high-pitched ringing in my ears, and my vision blurred, but everything soon returned to normal so I thought it didn´t really matter and I decided to ignored it.

I opened the door expecting to see the usual hallway, the same one I´d walked through so many times, but it wasn´t there. 

My eyes opened wide as I saw a seemingly ''endless flat grass landscape extending beyond where my view can perceive.''

I blinked twice, and when I looked back, the bedroom was gone, replaced by the same field.

I stood there in disbelief. I rubbed my eyes, pinched my arm, bit my hand. ''I wasn't dreaming''... 

My legs were shaking, and I started to slowly move forward.

But it´s always the same field. No matter how far I walk, no matter which direction I take, the landscape never truly changes. Just grass, the same blue sky, and the same wind.

The sun never moves. It just hangs there, fixed on one side of the sky like a painting. And on the other side, there´s a massive structure.

I don´t really know what it is, but it looks like some sort of bridge.

...

{{Collapse|initial=''It´s truly massive...''|final=''It´s truly massive..''.|body=

{{Image Banner|image=The Structure.jpg|caption=Will I ever reach it...?}}

Over time, I noticed something strange... I don´t age here. I don´t feel hunger. I don´t get thirsty. I don´t need to sleep, although sometimes I do manage to drift off, only to wake up in the same place, surrounded by the same grass and sky.

It´s like this place is frozen in time.

...

A very long time has passed. Longer than I can measure. Longer than I think any human should be able to endure.

I’ve lost count of the steps I’ve taken... millions... billions... trillions...

And whenever I do manage to sleep, I always dream the same thing: I´m standing beneath the structure, and I find a door hidden somewhere. I reach for the handle and begin to open it... but I never get to see what´s behind the door, I always wake up before that moment.

...

Millions of years have passed since I arrived here.

I remember, during those first endless years, I decided to walk towards the sun.

I kept walking and walking until, somehow, I reached it...

The plain suddenly ended, but somehow I was still able to walk... and there it was, the yellow dwarf star, right in front of me.

It wasn´t hot, not cold either. There was no warmth, no radiation, no sound, just its glow.

I remember reaching out and touching it with my own hands. And when I did, I felt... nothing. Not heat, not cold... just nothing.

I looked down, and beneath it, there was only a white void stretching out forever.

I decided to turn away from it. And in that moment, I began my journey towards the huge structure on the other side of the field.

...

That was eons ago, and I´m still walking.

I can´t even see the sun anymore, it´s so far away from me now, but somehow the place is still illuminated.

But the structure ahead? It hasn´t changed. Not even slightly. It still looks as distant as the first time I saw it.

How massive must it be, to not have shifted even a single centimeter after all this time walking?

Sometimes it feels like I´ve made no progress at all.

But I keep going.

I don´t know why.

I just know that I have to.

{{Wavy|text=I must keep walking.}}

...

{{Collapse|num=2|initial=''I wonder what´s happening in the real world...''|final=''I wonder what´s happening in the real world...''|body=
{{Image Banner|image=360 F 428033314 rp018Hqe7ACJuSrPSli4gfPszvkiAJir.jpg|caption=Critical condition.}}
-Dispatcher- "911, what’s your emergency?"

- “My daughter! She’s on the floor! She´s not moving!”

-Dispatcher- “Ma’am, please stay calm. What happened?”

- “She hit her head on the floor! She’s bleeding a lot and isn’t responding!”

-Dispatcher- “Understood. Please tell me your location immediately.”

...

'''In the hospital, the waiting room is too white, too quiet.'''

'''She sits there, staring at nothing. Hands clenched in her lap. Mouth trembling, but no sound comes out.'''

'''A doctor walks in. His eyes say everything before he opens his mouth.'''

-Doctor- “I’m sorry. We did everything we could.”

- "No… no, that’s not…"

-Doctor- "The trauma to her head... it was too severe. She never regained consciousness."

...

'''The house is too quiet when she returns.'''

'''In the kitchen, the dinner she was making is still there on the stove.'''

- "You were the last thing I had left..."

'''She opens the drawer in her bedroom. Takes out the pistol. It feels heavier than she remembered.'''

'''She sits on the bed, the same bed where she used to fold clothes and listen to giggles from the next room.'''

'''She places the barrel in her mouth.'''

'''Click. Click. Click.'''

'''Silence.'''

'''She opens her eyes slowly, breathing hard.'''

- "It... ''it didn´t shoot''..." '''Sobbing.''' "Maybe this is not the way... God gave me a second chance."

'''She wipes the tears from her face and slowly gets up.'''

'''She walks to the bedroom door, opens it and freezes. The hallway is gone, in its place, ''an endless flat grass landscape apparently leading to somewhere beyond the horizon.'''''

"...What is this?"
}}
}}
}}
